title: Software Stocks Are Sinking In 2026, But These 'Fab Five' Are Up 30% — And BofA Says They're Just Getting Started
author: Piero Cingari
description: Bank of America identifies five enterprise software stocks—Datadog, JFrog, MongoDB, Snowflake, and Twilio—as outperformers in 2026, collectively up 30% while the broader software sector declines 12%. The analyst attributes their strength to solid execution, AI exposure, and differentiated products, with AI-related revenue growing over 100% at Datadog and strong adoption across all five companies. BofA maintains Buy ratings on all five, expecting the gap between these winners and struggling peers to widen.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Surged 58% post-earnings with 32% YoY revenue growth acceleration. AI business growing over 100% annually and representing over 10% of revenue. Maintained Buy rating by BofA analyst.
Advanced 43% post-earnings with cloud revenue growth reaching 50%, up from 42% prior quarter. Increasing adoption among AI-focused customers. Maintained Buy rating.
Rose 5% post-earnings with Atlas revenue growth at 29.4%. Reported increasing adoption among AI-focused customers. BofA remains confident in long-term outlook with Buy rating maintained.
Climbed 37% post-earnings with product revenue growth accelerating to 34% YoY. AI products (Cortex AI and Cortex Code) driving growth. Maintained Buy rating.
Gained 38% post-earnings with gross-profit-dollar growth improving to 16% from 10%. Momentum from voice AI products. Maintained Buy rating.
Down 12% in 2026, representing the broader software sector decline that contrasts sharply with the outperformance of the 'Fab Five' stocks.

title: MongoDB Is the Latest SaaS Apocalypse Victim to Say ‘Not Today’
author: Sam Quirke
description: MongoDB has recovered from a 40% decline since January after delivering strong Q1 earnings that beat expectations and raised full-year guidance. The company's Atlas cloud database showed robust growth, and analysts now view AI as an opportunity rather than a threat, with MongoDB positioned as an 'essential database for AI' for legacy modernization and AI workload scaling. Multiple Wall Street firms reiterated Buy ratings with price targets up to $475, suggesting 20% upside from current levels.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong Q1 earnings beat with revenue and EPS exceeding expectations, raised full-year guidance well above Street estimates, Atlas showing strong year-over-year growth with lifted guidance, forward pipeline surged indicating robust demand visibility, and multiple analyst upgrades with price targets suggesting 20% upside. AI reframed as opportunity rather than threat for the company's business model.
Mentioned as a peer that successfully recovered from AI-related selloff fears with strong earnings, establishing a pattern of market repricing after initial pessimism proved unfounded.
Referenced as another SaaS company that recovered from AI disruption fears with strong earnings performance, following the same recovery pattern as MongoDB.
Mentioned as beginning to follow the recovery pattern of other SaaS companies that initially faced AI-related selloffs but are showing signs of recovery.

title: 9 Software Stocks That Could Thrive as AI Drives Enterprise Spending
author: David Wagner
description: The US software sector rallied strongly on June 1, 2026, with 14 large-cap software stocks gaining over 10%, led by MongoDB's 20.4% jump. Strong earnings reports from companies like Snowflake and Salesforce challenged fears that AI would reduce enterprise software demand, instead showing AI is becoming a major growth driver. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang reinforced this view, stating it's 'an incredible time to be a software company.' The article identifies undervalued software stocks with over 25% upside potential according to analyst estimates.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Reported strong Q1 results with revenue of $687.6M beating expectations of $664.5M, Atlas cloud platform revenue up 29.4% YoY, and management raised full-year outlook. Stock jumped 20.4%.
Delivered strong earnings report in late May that challenged 'SaaSpocalypse' fears and demonstrated AI as a growth driver for enterprise software.
Reported quarterly revenue of $11.13B up 13% YoY, announced $5B strategic investment in Anthropic, and stock rose 9.7%.
Climbed 12% as Nvidia's Computex event highlighted growing demand for agentic AI running on its network infrastructure.
Using AI to improve efficiency with 95% of engineers using AI coding tools and over 10% of code generated by AI. Strong Q1 2026 results with gross bookings up 25% and adjusted EBITDA up 33%.
Expanding SaaS business while integrating AI into offerings, including acquisition of Document Crunch for AI-powered construction document analysis.
CEO Jensen Huang reinforced bullish outlook for software companies during Computex keynote, arguing agentic AI will increase software demand rather than replace it.

title: S&P 500 Halts Gains As Oil Jumps 8% On Iran Ceasefire Standoff: Stock Market Today
author: Piero Cingari
description: Crude oil surged nearly 8% to its best day since April following U.S.-Iran military escalation, causing Treasury yields to rise and stalling the S&P 500's record run. The benchmark held flat while energy stocks led gains and software/AI stocks rallied on Nvidia's RTX Spark announcement. FedEx plunged 18.5% due to a mechanical spin-off, while MGM Resorts jumped 15.2% on an IAC takeover bid.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock rose 4.4% following announcement of RTX Spark superchip, which drove broad re-rating across enterprise software and AI workloads
Rallied 9.8% as part of SaaS/software sector strength driven by AI efficiency narrative and Nvidia's RTX Spark announcement
Gained 10.1% riding the sector-wide AI-software re-rating wave
Rose 9.7% as part of software sector rally driven by AI workload efficiency narrative
Surged 9.3% as legacy tech benefited from AI-software sector strength
Soared 16.5% as a top Russell 1000 gainer riding the sector-wide AI-software re-rating
Jumped 17.4% as a top gainer riding the AI-software sector re-rating wave
Fell 9.9% after posting Q1 revenue of $14.7 million versus $36.6 million estimate, significantly missing expectations
Dropped 14.0% despite Q1 revenue beat due to Blue Origin New Glenn explosion stoking sector-wide launch concerns and profit-taking
Sank 7.6% as Nvidia's RTX Spark superchip overshadowed its own Dragonfly AI launch and raised doubts about Snapdragon PC dominance
Surged 15.2% following IAC's all-cash takeover bid of $48.30 per share valuing the company at roughly $18 billion
Plunged 18.5% due to mechanical impact of spin-off of FedEx Freight unit, not company fundamentals
Plunged 18.5% due to mechanical impact of spin-off of FedEx Freight unit, not company fundamentals

title: Consumer Tech News (May 25-29): Dell, IBM, CrowdStrike, Meta Lead Big Tech AI Push
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Major tech companies drove AI innovation this week with strong earnings and strategic initiatives. Dell reported record Q1 revenue of $43.84B, significantly beating estimates, while IBM and Red Hat launched a $5B open-source security initiative. Meta expanded into paid subscriptions across its platforms, and CrowdStrike expanded its cybersecurity initiatives. Anthropic surpassed OpenAI as the world's most valuable startup at $965B valuation. Japanese banks gained access to OpenAI's GPT-5.5 model for cybersecurity defense.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Reported Q1 revenue of $43.84B, significantly exceeding analyst estimates of $35.45B, with adjusted EPS of $4.86 vs. expected $2.94. Secured a $9.7B Pentagon contract for Microsoft software licensing management.
Launched Project Lightwell, a $5B initiative to secure open-source software against AI-driven cyber threats, demonstrating strategic investment in emerging security needs.
Expanding Project QuiltWorks initiative with major cyber insurance industry players, strengthening market position in cybersecurity solutions.
Introducing paid subscriptions across Instagram, Facebook, WhatsApp, and Meta AI to unlock new AI-driven revenue streams and deepen monetization.
Reported Q1 earnings of $2.99 per share beating consensus of $2.84, and revenue of $1.93B exceeding estimates of $1.89B.
Q1 revenue of $11.13B beat consensus of $11.05B, with adjusted EPS of $3.88 significantly exceeding estimates of $3.12.
Q1 revenue of $687.62M beat estimates of $667.82M, with adjusted EPS of $1.32 exceeding consensus of $1.18.
Launched new Arc G-series processors for Windows handheld gaming PCs, expanding product portfolio in competitive gaming market.
CEO announced plans to spend approximately $150B annually in Taiwan, demonstrating strong commitment to AI infrastructure expansion.
Launched unified agentic AI capabilities for developing reliable AI agents, positioning itself in growing AI infrastructure market.

Q1 earnings of 15 cents missed Street estimate of 16 cents, though revenue of $418.38M beat consensus of $397.81M.
Q1 revenue of $421.3M exceeded analyst consensus estimate of $417.9M.

Non-GAAP net income of 42 cents per diluted share missed estimated 54 cents per share.

Reclaimed top spot in global BEV market during Q1 2026 according to Counterpoint Research, despite modest overall industry growth.
Federal court granted preliminary approval of settlement resolving two longstanding shareholder derivative lawsuits.
Successfully passed System Requirements Review for Space Development Agency's Tracking Layer Tranche 3 constellation contract worth approximately $816M.

Launched new AI-powered finance tools enabling autonomous agents to trade stocks and make purchases on behalf of users.
